Quick note on what robots.txt actually does, why it matters for SEO, and how to write one. Covers the basic directives (User-agent, Disallow, Allow, Sitemap), the most common crawler User-agents, and the important caveat — robots.txt is a convention, not a guarantee, so don't rely on it to hide sensitive info.
Vue 3 Two-Way Binding: Concept and Implementation
A quick walkthrough of Vue 3 two-way binding, including a small implementation.
Homebrew: homebrew-core / homebrew-cask Is a Shallow Clone — How to Fix It

Recently when installing Homebrew, I started seeing "homebrew-core is a shallow clone." and "homebrew-cask is a shallow clone." errors during install. This post records the fix — two git fetch --unshallow commands you need to run manually to make the install complete.
JavaScript Core Concepts (59) - ES6: let and const - The Basics

Time for a brand new chapter. From here on we start digging into ES6 syntax, beginning with let and const. We'll look at why var causes trouble with hoisting, redeclaration, and function scope, and how let and const fix all of that with block scope.
JavaScript Core Concepts (58): Where Property Descriptors Actually Show Up in Real Frameworks

We've spent a whole chapter on object property descriptors, so let's see where they actually matter in the real world. In this post I walk through Vue.js source code to show how Object.defineProperty powers two-way data binding — and why that makes property descriptors such an important concept to understand.
Vue 2 Two-Way Binding: Concept and Implementation

A quick note on how Vue implements v-model, and what implementing something similar from scratch (without Vue) would look like. Focused on Vue 2 — long-ish post since the focus is on implementation details.
Window Went Off-Screen on Mac or Windows? How to Pull It Back

Sometimes a window drifts off your screen and you can't grab it with the mouse. This post covers two quick fixes — Alt + Space → Move + arrow keys on Windows to drag any off-screen window back, and the Window → Zoom menu (or force-quit) on Mac.
JavaScript Core (57): Getter and Setter — Assigning a Value Without Calling a Function

Next up is Getter and Setter. If you've already started learning Vue, this concept is going to be especially useful to you. We'll look at how to define getters and setters directly in an object literal, how to do the same thing with Object.defineProperty, and where this pattern actually shows up in the real world (hint: Vue's two-way binding).
JavaScript Core (56) - Property Enumeration and the Prototype: Why for...in Leaks Prototype Props

Time to circle back to prototypes and the enumeration stuff we've been talking about, because the two are tangled together. In this post I'll walk through how property enumeration and the prototype chain relate, why for...in surprisingly pulls in prototype properties, and how to stop it using Object.defineProperty or hasOwnProperty.
Mac: "zsh: command not found: xxx" — Two Common Causes and Fixes

Hit "zsh: command not found: hexo" or similar on Mac after installing a global npm package? Two flavors of this issue, both common — one if you use nvm (npm prefix conflicting with nvm), one if you don't (zsh PATH not picking up npm's global bin directory). This post covers both fixes step by step.
